data.title: U.S. Employment Report to Influence Dollar's Trajectory
data.description: According to Odaily, an analyst from ING, Turner, has highlighted the significance of the upcoming U.S. non-farm employment report in determining whether the recent downward trend of the dollar will persist. U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell has indicated that persistent inflation and a robust labor market suggest that interest rates should remain at restrictive levels. Turner noted that any unexpected downturn in the employment report could undermine Powell's position and increase market expectations for a rate cut at the July meeting. He added that unless the employment data is weaker than anticipated, the dollar is likely to continue consolidating ahead of the July 4th U.S. holiday.

data.title: YZi Labs announces investment in Digital Asset
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, YZi Labs announced today that it has invested in Digital Asset, an enterprise-level tool platform that builds compliance solutions for regulated markets. The investment is based on Digital Asset's $135 million Series E financing announced on June 24, with participation from major financial institutions including DRW, Tradeweb, DTCC, Citadel Securities and Goldman Sachs. The funds will enable Digital Asset to expand the Canton Network's infrastructure, absorb new institutions more quickly, and support the composability and interoperability required to bring billions of dollars of real-world assets on-chain.

data.title: Binance Introduces Upgrades to Pay Send Service for Enhanced Crypto Transfers
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform has unveiled two significant upgrades to its Binance Pay Send service, aimed at streamlining the process of sending cryptocurrencies. These enhancements, Send via Contact and Send On-Chain, are designed to cater to diverse user preferences, offering improved speed, security, and convenience.

Binance Pay Send via Contact allows users to send crypto instantly to phone contacts without the need to input wallet addresses, emails, or Binance IDs. This feature supports over 300 cryptocurrencies and eliminates gas fees, providing a time-saving solution for users. By selecting a contact from their phone book within the Binance App, users can send crypto with minimal effort, reducing errors associated with manual entry of payment details. This upgrade enhances social connectivity by enabling users to connect with friends and family, making transfers between Binance users more intuitive.

The Send On-Chain feature facilitates crypto transfers by scanning a QR code or capturing a photo of a wallet address, eliminating manual input and network complexities. Utilizing AI-powered OCR technology, this feature automatically extracts payment details from images or screenshots, speeding up transactions and minimizing errors. It highlights character similarities and provides secondary confirmation for new addresses, ensuring enhanced accuracy and security. Additionally, this upgrade supports the widespread adoption of stablecoins by simplifying on-chain transactions.

Binance Pay Send is not available in certain restricted countries or regions due to legal and regulatory requirements. Users are advised to check the availability of these services in their region. The announcement emphasizes that discrepancies may exist between the original English content and translated versions, urging users to refer to the English version for the most accurate information.

data.title: U.S. Stablecoin Policy May Disrupt Global Payment Systems, Says AMUNDI
data.description: According to PANews, AMUNDI, Europe's largest asset management firm, has expressed concerns that the United States' stablecoin policy could potentially disrupt the global payment systems. This statement was reported by CGTN America. AMUNDI's analysis highlights the significant impact that U.S. regulatory decisions on stablecoins might have on international financial transactions and stability.

data.title: Robinhood CEO: OpenAI and SpaceX tokenized stocks are derivatives, not equity
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev said, "Tokenized stock promotion is the seed of a larger vision. These tokens are not technically equivalent to stocks, but they still allow retail investors to track changes in company valuations. Users can see the specific mechanism in our terms." The brokerage's official website also clearly pointed out that these "stock tokens" are not real company equity, but a blockchain-based derivative contract whose price tracks the market value changes of real stocks. 
As previously reported, OpenAI officially issued a statement denying that it authorized Robinhood to launch related tokenized stocks. The so-called "OpenAI token" is not the company's equity, nor has it established any partnership with Robinhood or participated in endorsement-related projects. OpenAI emphasized that any transfer involving the company's equity must be officially approved, and the company has not yet approved any such transactions, and called on the public to remain vigilant. (The Block)

data.title: JPMorgan Chase expects the stablecoin market to reach $500 billion in 2028, far below optimistic forecasts
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News JPMorgan Chase released a research report predicting that the global stablecoin market will grow to US$500 billion in 2028, far lower than the US$1 trillion to US$2 trillion predicted by some institutions. The bank pointed out that 88% of the current demand for stablecoins comes from crypto-native activities (such as trading, DeFi mortgages), and only 6% is used in payment scenarios. The report shows that stablecoins are difficult to replace bank deposits or money funds on a large scale, mainly due to lack of returns and friction in fiat-crypto conversion. Analysts refuted the view that stablecoins are comparable to China's digital RMB or mobile payments, emphasizing the essential differences between the two in centralized models. It is worth noting that Standard Chartered Bank predicted in April that if the US GENIUS Act is passed, the supply of stablecoins may surge to US$2 trillion in 2028. JPMorgan Chase believes that stablecoins are more likely to maintain a moderate growth path led by crypto demand rather than achieve widespread payment applications. (CoinDesk)

data.title: ING: US employment data is key to the dollar's performance
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Dutch International analyst Turner said in a report that the upcoming US non-farm payrolls report will play an important role in determining whether the recent downward trend of the US dollar will continue further. Federal Reserve Chairman Powell believes that stubborn inflation and a solid labor market mean that interest rates should remain restrictive for now. "Any unexpected downside in the employment report will weaken his position and push the market to price in expectations of a rate cut at the July meeting." He said that unless the employment data is weaker than expected, the US dollar may continue to consolidate before the US holiday on July 4. (Jinshi)

data.title: JPMorgan Predicts Stablecoin Market Growth to $500 Billion by 2028
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, JPMorgan strategists have projected that the stablecoin market could expand to $500 billion by 2028. This forecast, released in a research report on Thursday, is considered conservative compared to some aggressive predictions that estimate the market could reach $1 trillion to $2 trillion within the same timeframe.

JPMorgan's analysis presents a cautious outlook on the growth trajectory of the stablecoin industry. The report highlights that the primary driver of stablecoin usage remains "crypto-native demand" rather than broader adoption in payment scenarios.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary warns: Tariffs may rise back to April 2 levels
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessant warned America's trading partners not to extend trade talks, saying that if no progress is made, tariffs could rise back to April 2 levels. Bessant revealed that he would meet with EU negotiators today. When asked about the outcome of the discussion, he simply said, "We'll see what happens with the EU." On the issue of U.S. debt, Bessant expressed confidence in market demand. He said "there is a lot of demand for U.S. Treasuries," and demand for U.S. Treasuries at home and abroad remains strong. He described the debt management process as "orderly."
Bessant also commented on interest rates, suggesting that "two-year Treasury bonds tell you that overnight interest rates are too high" and that "inflation expectations should fall back." He criticized the Federal Reserve, saying that the committee's judgment "seems a little biased." Regarding the operation of the Federal Reserve, Bessant said that "it is good for the Federal Reserve to control spending" and that he hopes to "see the Federal Reserve reasonably adjust the size of the budget." When asked if he wanted Federal Reserve Chairman Powell to resign, Bessant declined to give a specific response. He noted that there are "a lot of strong candidates for Federal Reserve Chairman" and mentioned the possibility of "filling two Federal Reserve board positions next year." (Golden Ten)

data.title: Ripple Applies for National Banking License Amid Crypto-Finance Convergence
data.description:
Ripple has officially submitted an application for a national banking license, signaling a significant step in its strategy to navigate the evolving landscape of finance as the convergence of cryptocurrency and traditional banking systems becomes increasingly pronounced. This move highlights Ripple's commitment to integrating its innovative blockchain technology with regulated financial services, aiming to provide enhanced solutions for cross-border payments and facilitate a more seamless experience for users in the rapidly changing world of digital finance.
source: https://voiceofcrypto.online/ampstories/web-stories/ripple-applies-for-national-banking-license-amid-crypto-finance-convergence

data.title: SlowMist: GitHub's popular Solana tool hides a trap for stealing coins
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of the SlowMist security team, on July 2, a victim said that he had used an open source project hosted on GitHub the day before - zldp2002/solana-pumpfun-bot, and then his encrypted assets were stolen. According to SlowMist analysis, in this attack, the attacker disguised himself as a legitimate open source project (solana-pumpfun-bot) to induce users to download and run malicious code. Under the cover of brushing up the popularity of the project, the user ran the Node.js project with malicious dependencies without any defense, resulting in the leakage of wallet private keys and theft of assets. The entire attack chain involves multiple GitHub accounts to operate in coordination, which expands the scope of dissemination, enhances credibility, and is extremely deceptive. At the same time, this type of attack uses social engineering and technical means, and it is difficult to fully defend within the organization. SlowMist recommends that developers and users be highly vigilant against GitHub projects of unknown origin, especially when it comes to wallet or private key operations. If you really need to run and debug, it is recommended to run and debug in an independent machine environment without sensitive data.

data.title: Security Alert: GitHub Project Exploited in Cryptocurrency Theft
data.description:
According to PANews, a security incident involving a GitHub-hosted open-source project has resulted in the theft of cryptocurrency assets. On July 2, a victim reported using the project named zldp2002/solana-pumpfun-bot, which led to the unauthorized access and theft of their digital assets. The SlowMist security team analyzed the attack, revealing that the perpetrators disguised the malicious code as a legitimate open-source project. This deception encouraged users to download and execute the harmful Node.js project, which contained malicious dependencies. As a result, users' wallet private keys were compromised, leading to asset theft.

The attack involved multiple GitHub accounts working in coordination, which expanded the reach and credibility of the malicious project, making it highly deceptive. This type of attack combines social engineering with technical methods, making it challenging to defend against even within organizations.

SlowMist advises developers and users to exercise extreme caution when dealing with unfamiliar GitHub projects, especially those involving wallet or private key operations. It is recommended to run and debug such projects in isolated environments without sensitive data to mitigate risks.

data.title: ARK Invest Introduces Structured ETFs with Downside Protection
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Eric Balchunas, a senior ETF analyst at Bloomberg, announced on social media that ARK Invest, led by Cathie Wood, has recently filed for structured ETFs, also known as buffered ETFs. These funds are similar to ARKK but feature a 50% downside protection limit. The upside, as understood, allows for gains beyond the initial 5% increase. Balchunas noted that this move indicates a strong market demand for products offering certainty in returns.

data.title: Bloomberg analyst: The market demand for products with “certain income structure” is very strong
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Eric Balchunas, senior ETF analyst at Bloomberg, wrote, "Cathie Wood's ARK Invest has just applied for some "structured" ETFs, also known as buffer ETFs. Basically, it is similar to ARKK, but with a 50% decline limit, and the upside (as I understand it) is that except for the initial 5% increase, all other gains can be obtained. In my opinion, this shows that the market demand for products such as "certainty income structure" is very strong."
